sentence: Here is the SOAP note based on the provided transcript:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, is a 7/10 in terms of pain severity, describing a pain in the back of his neck that started after a car accident a week ago.
The pain is not constant but worsens with movement.
He has tried ibuprofen with some relief.
The pain is not radiating to other areas such as the upper back or shoulders.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient denies any hearing problems, visual disturbances, or other symptoms.
He has a history of playing football, but no previous neck issues.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: Positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending.
No pain with palpation of the back or shoulders.
Test Results: X-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain due to whiplash from a car accident, which is causing his neck pain.
Problem list: Neck strain; suspected concussion (unknown status).
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for pain management.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to rest and avoid straining his neck.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any nerve impingement or other complications.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment after the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will be referred to a specialist for further evaluation and treatment after the MRI results are available.
